insight: In Zemora Voidbringer sets, opening with Double Conduit (DC) is considered a mistake even for P2, because the resource cut is too steep. Players who mirror Zemora openings with DC tend to lose, as the set rewards faster Zemora deployment over greedy early economy.
author: crash_overlord
context: amalloy asked whether P1 opening 11C to rush Zemora faster is viable; crash_overlord and f0x both pushed back on DC openings in Zemora sets.

insight: Grimbotch is highly rated competitively (tier 9 on a community ranked tier list) due to being dirt cheap for a 1/2 attacker, and the Doomed status is largely irrelevant because Grimbotches are used as expendable blockers or sacrificed strategically anyway.
author: kdansky
context: kdansky played against Grimbotch-heavy defense and gained firsthand appreciation for why the community considers them strong, after previously undervaluing them.

insight: Apollo combined with Iso Kronus was attempted as an unpredictable pressure strategy to force a surprise breach, but Grimbotch defenders effectively blocked the attack, demonstrating that cheap Doomed blockers can neutralize otherwise synergistic attacker combinations.
author: kdansky
context: kdansky recapped a match where Apollo+Iso Kronus pressure was neutralized by opponent's Grimbotches acting as efficient blockers.

insight: BottyMcBotFace is essentially Master Bot (7-second think time) with an additional rush database, which makes it marginally stronger than standard Master Bot in rush-heavy situations, but not significantly stronger overall.
author: f300xen
context: Discussion about bot difficulty tiers; f0x clarified the technical difference between BottyMcBotFace and Master Bot 7s.

insight: A player around 1800 Master rating noted they still occasionally lose to Master Bot when not playing carefully, indicating Master Bot's strength is high enough to punish inattentive play even from experienced players.
author: redzonex
context: Community discussion about the relative strength of Prismata bots for new players; redzonex provided personal context on MB's competitive level.

insight: Adept Bot handles rush strategies differently from Master Bot; a player reported being able to beat Master Bot consistently but losing to Adept Bot, suggesting Adept Bot may have stronger or more specialized anti-rush responses.
author: miuna6933
context: New player discussion about which bot to practice against; miuna6933 shared personal experience of Adept Bot being harder than expected against rush strategies.

insight: Wacky Bot plays completely randomly without any strategic logic and is not intended as a skill benchmark; it cannot even reliably purchase Drones, making it only useful for ego-boosting or entertainment rather than practice.
author: consensus
context: Multiple community members described Wacky Bot's behavior when a new player asked about practicing against bots; valgrim_the_demon noted Wacky Bot cannot drone, confirmed by supergreyman.
